title: Inspectors of standards and regulations
description: Examine, evaluate, and investigate eligibility for or conformity with laws and regulations governing contract compliance of licenses and permits, and perform other compliance and enforcement inspection and analysis activities not classified elsewhere. Inspect and investigate sources of pollution to protect the public and environment and ensure conformance with Federal, State, and local regulations and ordinances. Monitor and evaluate compliance with equal opportunity laws, guidelines, and policies to ensure that employment practices and contracting arrangements give equal opportunity without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, age, or disability. (Based on 45 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Prepare documents recording outcomes of court proceedings and other official reports as necessary.
Perform contract administration duties, assisting with bid openings or awarding contracts and manage licensing policies and procedures.
Answer inquiries from the public regarding judicial procedures, licensing, or regulations and provide assistance in completing forms.
Coordinate office tracking systems for correspondence, follow-up actions, and maintain vital records and accounts.
Prepare meeting agendas or packets of related information and record and edit minutes of meetings for distribution.
Prepare and issue court orders, such as probation or release documentation, ensuring compliance with laws and procedures.
Examine credentials, licenses, or permits for compliance with licensing requirements and review legal documents for adherence to laws.
Investigate claims or violations, and prepare reports on findings and actions taken during inspections or inquiries.
Inspect vehicles or equipment for compliance with safety standards and regulations, recording evidence of damage or repairs needed.
Conduct environmental inspections and tests to evaluate potential hazards, ensuring adherence to health and safety regulations.
Collect, analyze, and report environmental data, such as pollution emissions and atmospheric measurements, for compliance assessment.
Inspect active remediation sites to ensure compliance with environmental or safety policies, standards, or regulations.
Conduct environmental audits, inspections, or investigations of violations and analyze results to determine necessary measures for compliance.
Plan or supervise environmental studies to achieve compliance with environmental regulations in the operation, acquisition, or modification of facilities.
Prepare and present illustrated lectures, reports, or documentation to inform government or environmental groups on environmental issues.
Monitor, assess, and analyze environmental impacts of development activities, pollution, or land degradation and recommend means of prevention or control.
Review and implement environmental technical standards, guidelines, policies, and formal regulations to ensure compliance with requirements.
Provide scientific or technical guidance, support, or oversight to governmental agencies, environmental programs, or the public on environmental issues.
Gather and review climate-related studies and analyze data to determine environmental conditions and restoration needs.
Research sources of pollution to determine their effects on the environment and develop methods for pollution abatement or control.
Develop environmental management or restoration plans based on regulations, standards, and landowner goals for sites with pipelines or renewable energy facilities.
Create diagrams or prepare modeling involving ecological systems and geographic information systems (GIS) to communicate environmental planning.
Coordinate with central government land managers and other organizations to manage and protect environmental resources and ensure efficient program implementation.
Train staff or provide technical direction on environmental compliance and restoration techniques to ensure adherence to policies and regulations.
Conduct applied research and feasibility studies on environmental topics, such as waste control, pollution abatement, or ecosystem impacts.
Communicate scientific or technical environmental information through various means like workshops, briefings, and public hearings to promote compliance.
Monitor the implementation of environmental practices and provide advice to land users on compliance with regulations and conservation techniques.
Warn violators of infractions or penalties and report law or regulation violations to appropriate boards or agencies.
Confer with or interview officials, technical or professional specialists, or applicants to obtain information relevant to licensing decisions.
Compile, log, or record testing or operational data for review and further analysis, including preparing reports and maintaining inspection records.
Collect or prepare solid or fluid samples for analysis, determining sampling locations and methods for compliance testing.
Test and analyze samples to determine their content and characteristics, using laboratory apparatus or testing equipment.
Inspect establishments to verify that valid licenses or permits are displayed and that licensing standards are being upheld.
Investigate employment practices or alleged violations of laws to document and correct discriminatory factors, preparing reports related to equal opportunity complaints.
Monitor the implementation of guidelines for nondiscriminatory employment practices and provide technical assistance or training on these topics.
Examine permits, licenses, applications, and records to ensure compliance with licensing requirements and the legal implications of relevant regulations.
Investigate complaints and suspected violations regarding illegal dumping, pollution, pesticides, product quality, or labeling laws, and monitor follow-up actions.
Clear goods through customs and advise customers on import/export restrictions, tariff systems, and insurance requirements.
Prepare and process import/export documentation according to customs regulations and calculate duty and tariff payments owed.
Confer with officials in various agencies to facilitate clearance of goods through customs and quarantine.
Monitor or trace the location of goods to ensure compliance with laws and regulations governing contracts.
Review engineering plans and specifications to ensure adherence to codes and standards.
Inspect, diagnose, maintain, and operate test setups and equipment to detect malfunctions.
Conduct failure analyses, document results, and recommend corrective actions based on test data.
Analyze test results in relation to design specifications and modify equipment to meet requirements.
Prepare specifications and designs for machines and components related to mechanical, electrical, or electronic systems.
Select and procure parts and maintain inventory and documentation systems for compliance and regulation purposes.
Inspect the cleanliness and practices of establishment employees and ensure proper garbage disposal and lack of vermin infestation.
Inspect agricultural commodities or related operations, as well as fish and logging operations, for compliance with health, quality, and safety laws and regulations.
Inspect facilities or installations to determine their environmental impact and monitor construction activities to ensure adherence to environmental regulations.
Conduct inspections to verify safety, ensure compliance with specifications and regulations, and explain required standards to agricultural workers.
Inspect construction sites, bridges, dams, and other structures to ensure structural quality and compliance with safety specifications and codes.
Evaluate and monitor project details to ensure adherence to environmental regulations and that energy-efficient practices are upheld.
Prepare reports documenting inspections, project activities, energy analysis results, or recommendations for compliance and safety improvements.
Confer with owners, violators, or authorities to explain regulations, provide recommendations for remedial actions, and discuss safety issues.
Inspect work progress, equipment, and construction sites to verify compliance with plans, codes, and safety standards during project execution.
Train, direct, or supervise construction or inspection personnel to ensure adherence to safety regulations and compliance with project specifications.
Record information, such as personnel, production, or operational data on specified forms or reports.
Confer with managerial or technical personnel, other departments, or contractors to resolve problems or coordinate activities.
Inspect properties that store, handle, and use hazardous materials to ensure compliance with laws, codes, and regulations.
Review blueprints and plans for new or remodeled buildings to ensure the structures meet fire safety codes.
Conduct inspections and acceptance testing of newly installed fire protection systems.

Identify corrective actions necessary to bring properties into compliance with applicable fire codes, laws, regulations, and standards, and explain these measures to property owners or their representatives.
Prepare and maintain reports of investigation results and records of convicted arsonists and arson suspects.
Develop or review fire exit plans and participate in creating fire safety guidelines and evacuation schemes for nonresidential buildings.
Analyze evidence and other information to determine the probable cause of fire or explosion.
Inspect buildings to locate hazardous conditions and fire code violations, such as electrical wiring problems and non-functional fire exits.
Communicate fire details to superiors, subordinates, or interagency dispatch centers using two-way radios.
Monitor field work to ensure proper performance and use of materials, and inspect work areas to ensure compliance with established standards.
Enforce safety rules and regulations and take disciplinary action to address performance problems.
Confer with customers, supervisors, contractors, or other personnel to exchange information or resolve problems.
Plan work assignments and equipment allocations to meet transportation, operations or production goals.
Inspect materials, stock, vehicles, equipment, or facilities to ensure they are safe, free of defects, and consistent with specifications.
Analyze and record operational data and write related activity reports.
Train workers in proper operational procedures and company policies and explain work tasks to new workers.
Recommend and implement measures to improve worker motivation, work methods, or customer services.
Resolve worker problems or collaborate with employees to assist in problem resolution.
Observe and evaluate workers' appearance and performance to ensure quality service and compliance with specifications.
